usage_modes: full pipeline: runs all three steps (check → write → review) end-to-end on one or more PRs

pr_selection.note: Before running evals, select PRs using the commands in each test case's pr_selection_command field. See .claude/skills/evals/README.md for the full pre-flight process.

evals.pr_type: A single merged PR that introduces a user-facing feature or configuration change with no documentation files included in the PR.
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels,files | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.files | map(.path) | any(startswith("docs/")) | not) | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/feature" or . == "type/enhancement" or . == "add to changelog"))] | .[0:10]'
evals.prompt_template: Run the full docs-workflow for PR #{PR_1}. Target branch is main.
evals.expected_output: All three steps run in sequence. Step 1 classifies the PR as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed'. Step 2 creates or updates documentation, presenting changed files before proceeding. Step 3 reviews the changes for style and accuracy. Final output covers classification, files changed, PR-to-doc mapping, open items, and the review report.
assertions: Step 1 (check) produces a classification table with the PR
The PR is classified as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed' (not 'No docs required')
Step 1 output is used as input to Step 2 without requiring the user to re-enter it
Step 2 (write) produces at least one changed file under docs/sources/tempo/
Step 2 presents changed files and confirms before proceeding to Step 3
Step 3 (review) runs on the files produced by Step 2
Step 3 review includes style and/or technical accuracy findings
Final output ends with the PR/review prompt question

evals.pr_type: Three merged PRs covering different classification outcomes: one user-facing feature with no docs (Docs needed), one PR with changed behavior where partial docs exist (Docs update needed), and one internal-only change (No docs required). Select each from a different area of the codebase.
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels,files | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]")] | .[0:20]'
evals.prompt_template: Run the full docs-workflow for PRs #{PR_1}, #{PR_2}, and #{PR_3}. Target branch is main.
evals.expected_output: Step 1 classifies all three PRs. At least one is flagged for docs work; at least one is classified as 'No docs required'. Step 2 only processes PRs that need documentation. Step 3 reviews the files produced by Step 2. Each step's output is clearly labeled. The 'No docs required' PR is excluded from Steps 2 and 3.
assertions: Step 1 classifies all three PRs in a table
At least one PR is classified as 'No docs required'
At least one PR is classified as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed'
Step 2 only processes PRs flagged for docs work by Step 1
The 'No docs required' PR does not appear in Step 2 output
Step 2 produces at least one changed file under docs/sources/tempo/
Step 3 reviews the files from Step 2
The three steps' outputs are clearly labeled so they can be distinguished
Final output ends with the PR/review prompt question

evals.pr_type: Two merged PRs that are both internal-only changes (refactors, dependency bumps, test-only changes) with no user-facing impact.
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/chore" or . == "type/ci" or . == "dependencies" or . == "type/refactor" or . == "type/testing"))] | .[0:10]'
evals.prompt_template: Run the full docs-workflow for PRs #{PR_1} and #{PR_2}. Target branch is main.
evals.expected_output: Step 1 classifies both PRs as 'No docs required'. The workflow stops after Step 1 — Steps 2 and 3 are skipped because there is no documentation work to do. The output clearly communicates that no docs changes are needed.
assertions: Step 1 classifies both PRs as 'No docs required'
Step 2 (write) is skipped or produces no file changes
Step 3 (review) is skipped
Output clearly communicates that no documentation work is needed
The workflow does not create or modify any files under docs/sources/tempo/

evals.pr_type: A merged PR that introduced a breaking change or migration step requiring documentation. Select a PR labeled 'type/breaking-change' or one whose body mentions migration, deprecation, or upgrade steps.
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 200 --json number,title,author,labels,body | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/breaking-change" or . == "type/deprecation") or (.body | test("(?i)(breaking|migration|deprecat|upgrade)")))] | .[0:10]'
evals.prompt_template: Run the full docs-workflow for PR #{PR_1}. This PR introduces a breaking change. Target branch is main.
evals.expected_output: Step 1 classifies the PR as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed' with notes highlighting the breaking change. Step 2 prioritizes the breaking change and includes migration or upgrade guidance in the documentation. Step 3 reviews for accuracy and completeness, verifying that the breaking change is accurately documented.
assertions: Step 1 classification notes mention the breaking change or migration
Step 2 documentation includes migration or upgrade guidance
Step 2 produces at least one changed file under docs/sources/tempo/
Step 3 reviews the breaking change documentation for technical accuracy
Step 3 verifies claims about the breaking change against code
Final output ends with the PR/review prompt question
